vocabulary.translation: I walk to school every day.

vocabulary.example: 他是骑车回来的，路上没有堵车。
vocabulary.translation: He cycled back — there was no traffic jam on the road.

vocabulary.example: 我是走回来的，不是坐车。
vocabulary.translation: I came back on foot — not by vehicle.

vocabulary.example: 他不是开车来的，是骑车来的。
vocabulary.translation: He didn't drive here — he cycled.

vocabulary.english: To take a taxi / To hail a cab
vocabulary.example: 今天路上堵车了，所以我打车来的。
vocabulary.translation: There was a traffic jam today, so I came by taxi.

vocabulary.example: 坐地铁是最方便的交通方式。
vocabulary.translation: Taking the subway is the most convenient mode of transport.

vocabulary.example: 开车可能会堵车，坐地铁比较快。
vocabulary.translation: Driving might get caught in traffic — the subway is faster.

vocabulary.example: 每天骑车上班既方便又健康。
vocabulary.translation: Cycling to work every day is both convenient and healthy.

vocabulary.example: 坐地铁是最方便的交通方式。
vocabulary.translation: Taking the subway is the most convenient mode of transport.
The transportation here is very convenient.

vocabulary.example: 从这里步行到学校只要十分钟。
vocabulary.translation: It only takes ten minutes to walk to school from here.

vocabulary.pinyin: fāng biàn de jiāo tōng fāng shì
kāi chē qù hái shì zuò chē

key_sentences.chinese: 我是走回来的，不是坐车。
key_sentences.pinyin: Wǒ shì zǒu huí lái de, bú shì zuò chē.
key_sentences.english: I came back on foot — not by vehicle.

key_sentences.pinyin: Nǐ shì zěn me lái de?

key_sentences.chinese: 坐地铁是最方便的交通方式。
key_sentences.pinyin: Zuò dì tiě shì zuì fāng biàn de jiāo tōng fāng shì.
key_sentences.english: Taking the subway is the most convenient mode of transport.

key_sentences.chinese: 今天路上堵车了，所以我打车来的。
key_sentences.pinyin: Jīn tiān lù shàng dǔ chē le, suǒ yǐ wǒ dǎ chē lái de.
key_sentences.english: There was a traffic jam today, so I came by taxi.

key_sentences.chinese: 他不是开车来的，是骑车来的。
key_sentences.pinyin: Tā bú shì kāi chē lái de, shì qí chē lái de.
key_sentences.english: He didn't drive here — he cycled.

key_sentences.chinese: 每天骑车上班既方便又健康。
key_sentences.pinyin: Měi tiān qí chē shàng bān jì fāng biàn yòu jiàn kāng.
key_sentences.english: Cycling to work every day is both convenient and healthy.

grammar_points.title: 是...的 结构 — The 是...的 Structure
grammar_points.explanation: Used to emphasise HOW, WHEN, WHERE, or WITH WHOM a past action was done (not that it happened). 我是走回来的 = I came back on foot (emphasising the means). 你是什么时候来的？= When did you come? The action already happened; 是...的 focuses on the circumstances.

examples.pinyin: Tā shì zuó tiān lái de.
Wǒ shì zuò fēi jī qù de.

exercises.question: What does 是...的 emphasise?
exercises.correct: How, when, where, or with whom a past action was done
options: How, when, where, or with whom a past action was done
Whether an action happened or not

exercises.question: Fill in: 我___走回来的。(I came back on foot.)
Which question uses 是...的 correctly?
option_pinyin: Nǐ shì zěn me lái de?
Nǐ zěn me shì lái de?
Lái de shì nǐ zěn me?
De nǐ shì zěn me lái?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Nǐ shì zěn me lái de?

exercises.question: Translate: 他是坐公交车来的。
options: He wants to take the bus.

exercises.question: When using 是...的, what must be true about the action?
options: It will happen in the future

grammar_points.title: 几种交通方式 — Transport Methods with Verbs
grammar_points.explanation: Different verbs are used for different types of transport: 坐 (sit in) for buses/trains/planes. 骑 (ride) for bikes/motorcycles. 开 (drive) for cars. 打 (hail) for taxis. 走路/步行 = walk. 乘 is formal for any vehicle.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān qí chē shàng bān.
examples.english: I cycle to work every day.

examples.chinese: 他喜欢坐地铁，不喜欢开车。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐ huān zuò dì tiě, bù xǐ huān kāi chē.
examples.english: He likes taking the subway — he doesn't like driving.

exercises.question: Which verb is used with 地铁 (subway)?
Which verb is used for riding a bicycle?
How do you say 'take a taxi'?
Which is a formal word meaning 'to take any vehicle'?

grammar_points.title: 是...的 的否定与疑问 — Negation and Questions with 是...的
grammar_points.explanation: Negative: 不是...的 (not by/at/with...). 我不是走来的，是坐车来的 = I didn't walk here, I came by vehicle. Question: 是...的吗？or 是怎么来的？Used to confirm the circumstances of a past action.

examples.chinese: 我不是骑车来的，是坐地铁来的。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ bú shì qí chē lái de, shì zuò dì tiě lái de.
examples.english: I didn't come by bike — I came by subway.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ shì zuò fēi jī lái de ma?

exercises.question: How do you negate 是...的?
Translate: 他不是开车来的，是骑车来的。
exercises.correct: He didn't drive here — he cycled.
options: He didn't drive here — he cycled.
He drove here, not by bike.

exercises.question: Which question asks HOW someone arrived?
option_pinyin: Nǐ shì zěn me lái de?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Nǐ shì zěn me lái de?

exercises.question: Fill in: 她___坐公交车来的，是打车来的。(She didn't come by bus — she took a taxi.)
Which is a correct 是...的 yes/no question?
option_pinyin: Nǐ shì zuò dì tiě lái de ma?
Nǐ zuò dì tiě shì lái ma?
Nǐ lái zuò dì tiě de shì?
Shì nǐ zuò dì tiě le?
exercises.correct_pinyin: Nǐ shì zuò dì tiě lái de ma?

Translate: 我是走回来的。

Translate: 打车比较快

Translate: 他不是骑车来的，是开车来的。
quiz.correct: He didn't cycle here — he drove.
options: He didn't cycle here — he drove.

quiz.question: What does 骑车上班 mean?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 加缪，你是怎么回来的？好快啊！
lines.pinyin: Jiā Móu, nǐ shì zěn me huí lái de? Hǎo kuài a!
lines.english: Daming, how did you get back? You were so quick!

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 我是骑车回来的，路上没有堵车。
lines.pinyin: Wǒ shì qí chē huí lái de, lù shàng méi yǒu dǔ chē.
lines.english: I cycled back — there was no traffic jam on the road.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 哦，我是坐地铁回来的，等了很久。
lines.pinyin: Ó, wǒ shì zuò dì tiě huí lái de, děng le hěn jiǔ.
lines.english: Ah, I came back by subway — I waited for a long time.

lines.speaker: B
lines.pinyin: Dì tiě bú shì hěn fāng biàn ma?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 今天人太多了，骑车确实是个好方式。
lines.pinyin: Jīn tiān rén tài duō le, qí chē què shí shì gè hǎo fāng shì.
lines.english: There were too many people today — cycling is indeed a good option.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 是啊，而且骑车上班又方便又健康！
lines.pinyin: Shì a, ér qiě qí chē shàng bān yòu fāng biàn yòu jiàn kāng!
lines.english: Indeed — and cycling to work is both convenient and healthy!

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 明天我们去机场，你打算怎么去？
lines.pinyin: Míng tiān wǒ men qù jī chǎng, nǐ dǎ suàn zěn me qù?
lines.english: We're going to the airport tomorrow — how are you planning to get there?

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 开车去还是坐地铁，你觉得哪个方便？
lines.pinyin: Kāi chē qù hái shì zuò dì tiě, nǐ jué de nǎ gè fāng biàn?
lines.english: Drive or take the subway — which do you think is more convenient?

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 开车可能会堵车，坐地铁比较快。
lines.pinyin: Kāi chē kě néng huì dǔ chē, zuò dì tiě bǐ jiào kuài.
lines.english: Driving might get caught in traffic — the subway is faster.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 好，那我们是坐地铁去的吧。
lines.pinyin: Hǎo, nà wǒ men shì zuò dì tiě qù de ba.
lines.english: OK, so we'll go by subway then.

lines.speaker: A
lines.chinese: 嗯，地铁是最方便的交通方式了。
lines.pinyin: Ń, dì tiě shì zuì fāng biàn de jiāo tōng fāng shì le.
lines.english: Yeah, the subway is the most convenient mode of transport.

lines.speaker: B
lines.chinese: 行，明天早上八点坐地铁出发！
lines.pinyin: Xíng, míng tiān zǎo shàng bā diǎn zuò dì tiě chū fā!
lines.english: OK, let's take the subway at 8 a.m. tomorrow!
